auth.scheme: DD-API-KEY and DD-APPLICATION-KEY headers on every Datadog request; keys never go in the query string and are NEVER forwarded to presigned part-upload URLs

create_upload.body: {data: {type: 'upload', attributes: {table_name, headers (CSV columns in file order), part_count, part_size}}}
create_upload.success: 201 with {data: {id: upload_id, type: 'upload', attributes: {part_urls: [presigned URLs, expire after 5 minutes]}}}
create_upload.limits: part_count max 20; every part except the last must be at least 5,000,000 bytes

upload_parts.body: the raw CSV bytes for that part
upload_parts.note: presigned storage URLs — no Datadog auth headers

create_table.body: {data: {type: 'reference_table', attributes: {table_name, source: 'LOCAL_FILE', schema: {fields: [{name, type}], primary_keys: [one field name]}, file_metadata: {upload_id}, description?, tags?}}}
create_table.success: 201 with the table document; processing continues asynchronously

get_table.success: 200 with {data: {id, type: 'reference_table', attributes: {table_name, status, row_count, schema, source, file_metadata?}}}

replace_rows.body: {data: {id, type: 'reference_table', attributes: {file_metadata: {upload_id: <NEW upload>}}}} — source cannot change after creation and schema is not resent for a row replace

list_tables.query: page[limit] (default 15) and page[offset] (default 0) offset pagination; optional sort (default -updated_at), filter[status], filter[table_name][exact|contains]
list_tables.success: 200 with {data: [table documents]}; iterate by advancing page[offset] by page[limit] until a short page

schema_rules.primary_keys: list of field names, but only ONE primary key is supported; it is used as the row ID
schema_rules.primary_key_membership: the primary key must be one of the schema fields

async_processing.status_field: attributes.status; 'DONE' is ready, 'PROCESSING' means poll again, 'ERROR' is terminal failure (fixture status strings)
async_processing.errors: on failure attributes.file_metadata carries error_message, error_row_count, and error_type

test_fixture_notes.poll: waitUntilReady(tableId, pollIntervalMillis, maxPolls): at most maxPolls GETs with an injected sleep between polls
